user.name: Sergey Levine

fullText: Diffusion (or flow) makes for excellent policies, but training them with RL is notoriously hard: BPTT is unstable, RL over diffusion blows up the horizon. In our new paper, we show how we can optimize flow matching actors by using "one weird trick" -- "approximate" the Jacobian of the flow denoising process with the identity matrix. 👇

fullText:
MEET THE WOLF PACK ROBOTS TRANSFORMING JD LOGISTICS

JD logistics has a series of robots called Langzu (wolf pack).

The idea is for swift, coordinated pack animals.

Here are 4 of them:

1: Tianlang (天狼 - Sky Wolf)

A high-speed shuttle system for multi-tier, high-density tote storage and rapid container retrieval.  

2: Dilang (地狼 - Earth Wolf)

A low-profile AGV (Automated Guided Vehicles) that lifts shelves and carries pallets across warehouse floors.

3: Zhilang (智狼 - Intelligent Wolf)

A modular Goods-to-Person (GTP) solution that combines handling AGVs and lifting robots

These can leverage vertical spaces up to 10 meters high, tripling traditional picking speeds.  

4: Yilang (意朗 - Intuitive Wolf)

Robotic arms equipped with force sensors and machine vision.

These are optimized for sorting irregular parcels or folding boxes at high speed.

user.name: Natasha Jaques
user.description: Assistant Professor leading the Social RL Lab https://t.co/ykwfJG84Bj @uwcse and Staff Research Scientist at @GoogleAI.

fullText:
Really enjoyed reading the Microsoft MAI-Thinking-1 "Building a Hill Climbing Machine" paper. Amazing they publicly released all the info needed to train a frontier model, down to hparams.

I also thought this was pretty telling:
- pre-training: 30 trillion tokens
- mid-training (SFT on STEM/math/code data): 3.55 trillion tokens
- RL post-training: 150 billion tokens.
Looks like @ylecun was right all along with the cake analogy.

Obviously I still think something like RL (optimizing for long term goals) is fundamental to what we think of as intelligence. But it's not the volume of learning signal, it's the optimization on top of an already reasonable predictive model.

user.name: Kirk Borne
user.description: Advisor to startups. Freelancer. @LeadershipData founder. Global Speaker. Top #B2B influencer & social promoter #DataScience #AI #ML. PhD Astrophysics @Caltech

fullText:
XGBoost for Regression, Predictive Modeling, and Time Series Analysis — Learn how to build, evaluate, & deploy predictive models:
https://t.co/DsGwnEXyWF v/ @PacktDataML
—
My review:

🟠XGBoost is definitely the focal point and central contribution of this book, along with all those other things: Regression Trees, Predictive Modeling, Time Series Analysis, and Forecasting. 

🟠This book’s added value comes in its thorough progression from start to finish with comprehensive coverage of all the major steps and issues in data science and machine learning modeling: data cleaning, imbalanced data, feature engineering, feature engineering pipeline, feature importance, categorical data encoding, model interpretability and explainability, ensembles, model training, model evaluation metrics, hyperparameter tuning, model deployment, and more - including a quick-start case study starter project that feeds into next-level projects. 

🟠I highly recommend this broadly useful book for all levels of practitioners.

fullText:
💳 Card fraud happens in seconds, but many detection systems still rely on slow batch processing or complex streaming architectures.

This open-source Solution Accelerator shows how teams can build an end-to-end fraud detection workflow with Spark Real-Time Mode and Lakebase, including low-latency ML scoring, explainable fraud signals, and live monitoring on a unified platform.

With it, organizations can achieve sub-second fraud intervention while reducing operational complexity and avoiding the need for separate streaming infrastructure. 

https://t.co/fNdmwMgVs3
